notes: Update on my previous report a few weeks back, this now appears to be running perfectly at least on Nvidia. No black screen issues, solid framerate, with only a slight dip upon planet reentry. Multiplayer appears to work well.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Doesn't allow binding any controls to joystick axes or buttons, but that might be a limitation of the game (who makes a game about piloting space fighters but doesn't support joysticks?) and not a Proton issue
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Setting "TextureStreamingEx" value to "Off" in Binaries/SETTINGS/TKGRAPHICSSETTINGS.MXML significantly reduces stuttering when flying or walking on planets -- the game bogs down paging textures in and out of VRAM constantly with the default "Auto" (seems like it doesn't detect VRAM size properly so it assumes a very low value and unloads textures as soon as you even look away).
title: No Man's Sky

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 with Max-Q Design
notes: Works great out of the box!
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Initially the game would crash on startup. After setting it to use Wine D3d11, it runs, but doesn't load any 3D graphics, only the main menu and the text (for example, the system names during the shader loading screen). Tried setting to windowed mode, but didn't help.
title: No Man's Sky

notes:
Game would refuse to open at first. Runs fine after adding the following to the Launch Options:
MESA_GL_VERSION_OVERRIDE=4.5COMPAT MESA_GLSL_VERSION_OVERRIDE=450 PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%

First two fix the white screen issue and the second gets the game to open. Played for multiple hours with zero issues.

title: No Man's Sky

notes: Runs great! Did not try on Windows after last update, but on Linux with Proton works very smooth with high frame-rate! With previous NMS updates I played on Windows and performance was much worse. I use maxed settings with 3840x2160 resolution and get 80-110FPS! Impressive! Honestly did not expected such a great performance. I think Hello Games improved well they game :) I will try to compare to Windows 10 performance soon
title: No Man's Sky

notes: On AMD cards you will need Mesa 18.3 or higher as the game needs OpenGL 4.5 support.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Was new to the game, started off on Normal mode, went through the tutorial quests to survive, analyze discoveries and eventually repair my ship.  Game systems all worked normally, performance was smooth and no problems were encountered during game play session lasting 2 hours.  OS running Kubuntu 18.10.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: No issues that I am aware of after 20 hours of game play.  Launches without issue, there is a load time for first launch after first install or any update, Building Shaders, yet to my understanding this is the same on that other "OS", so just give it time.  As a note on first load after install, when you see a white background with a "circled E", press and hold E to load into spawn, this is after Shader build.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Around 60 fps with some stuttering some times. But they are really rare. First time loading into the Game takes very very long. Sound have some minor cracking.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Works perfect

notes: Game runs perfectly out of the box, only cursor in steam overlay didnt work. Performance was okay and shader load times way faster than on windows.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Runs extremely well but does lose some frame rates and has more stuttering compared to running in Windows, even on my limited gen7 i3 processor.
